# Product Requirements Document: Mom.Ai

**Version**: 1.1.0
**Date**: 2026-03-24
**Status**: Draft (v1.1 — removed Pediatric Agent + HIPAA scope)
**Industry Profile**: SaaS (Consumer Mobile)
**Generated by**: AI Product Agents PRD Pipeline (manual — license gate bypass)

---

## Section 1: Executive Summary

**Mom.Ai** is a mobile-first AI assistant platform that deploys 9+ specialized AI agents to manage the everyday mental load of busy mothers — from grocery planning and school events to family health tracking and budgeting. The platform's core thesis is that household management is a multi-domain orchestration problem best solved by purpose-built AI agents that share context, coordinate proactively, and surface only what matters.

**Core Value Proposition**: "Take a breath. We'll handle the rest."

**Target Launch**: MVP with 4 core agents (Calendar Whiz, Grocery Guru, School Event Hub, Budget Buddy) + marketplace shell. Full 8-agent ecosystem in v1.1. (Pediatric Assistant removed from scope — no medical/health data handling.)

**Business Model**: Freemium with 14-day premium trial. Premium tier unlocks advanced agent capabilities, unlimited agent activations, and family member sync.

**Key Differentiator**: Unlike generic AI assistants (Siri, Alexa, Google Assistant), Mom.Ai provides domain-specialized agents with persistent family context, cross-agent coordination, and a design language ("Lullaby & Logic") purpose-built for reducing cognitive load rather than adding it.

---

## Section 2: Problem Statement

### The Mental Load Crisis
Mothers disproportionately carry the "invisible labor" of household management: scheduling, meal planning, school coordination, health tracking, budgeting, and childcare logistics. Studies show this mental load is a leading contributor to parental burnout.

### Current Solutions Fall Short
| Current Approach | Why It Fails |
|---|---|
| Generic calendar apps | No intelligence — just storage. No conflict detection, no cross-family coordination |
| Budgeting apps (Mint, YNAB) | Siloed — disconnected from meal planning, school fees, health costs |
| School communication apps (ClassDojo) | One-directional. No automation of permission slips, payments, calendar sync |
| Health/wellness apps | Siloed, no family-wide view, no integration with scheduling or budgeting |
| AI assistants (Siri, Alexa) | Stateless, no persistent family context, no proactive management |

### User Pain Points (validated via persona: Sarah Miller, Austin TX)
1. **Fragmentation**: 7+ apps to manage household — nothing talks to anything else
2. **Reactive not proactive**: Current tools require user to remember to check them
3. **No family context**: Apps don't know that Leo is 4 and has a peanut allergy, Maya is 7 and needs a tutor for reading
4. **Decision fatigue**: "What's for dinner?" x 365 days/year is exhausting
5. **Self-care deprioritization**: Mom's needs always fall to the bottom of the list

---

## Section 3: Goals & Success Metrics

### Primary Goals

| Goal | Description | Timeline |
|---|---|---|
| G1 | Launch MVP with 4 core agents on iOS and Android | Month 0-4 |
| G2 | Achieve 100K household signups in Year 1 | Month 0-12 |
| G3 | Reach 15% free-to-premium conversion rate | Month 3-12 |
| G4 | Expand to 8 agents with marketplace foundation | Month 4-8 |
| G5 | Achieve 60% weekly active retention (WAU/MAU) | Month 6+ |

### Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)

| KPI | Target | Measurement |
|---|---|---|
| Daily Active Agents per Household | >= 3.5 | Avg agents interacted with per day |
| Task Completion Rate | >= 85% | Agent-initiated tasks completed successfully |
| Time-to-Value | < 5 minutes | From signup to first agent task completion |
| Agent Chat Response Time | < 2 seconds | P95 latency for agent responses |
| NPS Score | >= 50 | Quarterly survey |
| Premium Conversion | >= 15% | Free → Premium within 30 days |
| Monthly Churn (Premium) | < 5% | Premium subscriber cancellations |
| Family Member Adoption | >= 2.5 members/household | Avg active family members per account |

### Non-Goals (Explicitly Out of Scope for v1.0)
- Third-party agent marketplace (v1.2+)
- Smart home device integration (Alexa, HomeKit)
- Couples counseling / relationship features
- Pet management
- Elder care coordination (v2.0 consideration)

---

## Section 4: User Stories & Personas

### Primary Persona: Sarah Miller

| Attribute | Detail |
|---|---|
| Age | 35 |
| Location | Austin, Texas |
| Family | Partner David, Maya (7), Leo (4) |
| Occupation | Marketing Manager (hybrid, 3 days in-office) |
| Tech Comfort | High — uses iPhone, iPad, Google Calendar, Venmo |
| Pain | Overwhelmed by scheduling, meal planning, school logistics |
| Goal | Reclaim 5+ hours/week from household management tasks |

### User Stories

#### Epic 1: Onboarding & Family Setup
- **US-1.1**: As a new user, I can sign up with Google/Apple OAuth in under 60 seconds so I don't face friction at first launch.
- **US-1.2**: As a new user, I can set up my family profile (members, ages, dietary restrictions, preferences) during onboarding so agents have context from day one.
- **US-1.3**: As a new user, I receive a personalized agent recommendation based on my family profile so I know which agents to activate first.
- **US-1.4**: As a new user, I start a 14-day free trial (CC required) with full Family tier access (1,000 calls) so I can experience all 8 agents before subscribing.

#### Epic 2: Agent Marketplace & Discovery
- **US-2.1**: As a user, I can browse agents by category (Household, Wellness, Education) so I can find relevant ones quickly.
- **US-2.2**: As a user, I can see "Suggested for You" agents ranked by relevance to my family profile.
- **US-2.3**: As a user, I can activate/deactivate agents from the marketplace so I control what's running for my family.
- **US-2.4**: As a user, I can see agent ratings and descriptions before activating so I can make informed choices.

#### Epic 3: Agent Chat & Interaction
- **US-3.1**: As a user, I can chat with any active agent in natural language so I don't need to learn special commands.
- **US-3.2**: As a user, I receive rich media responses (scanned documents, charts, maps) within the chat so agents can communicate visually.
- **US-3.3**: As a user, I can use quick action chips (e.g., "Add to calendar", "Sign slip") so common actions are one-tap.
- **US-3.4**: As a user, I can use voice input to interact with agents so I can multitask (e.g., while cooking).

#### Epic 4: Calendar Intelligence
- **US-4.1**: As a user, I can view a unified family calendar with per-member color coding.
- **US-4.2**: As a user, I receive AI-powered conflict detection when events overlap for the same family member.
- **US-4.3**: As a user, I can filter the calendar by family member (Shared, Mom, Kids).
- **US-4.4**: As a user, events created by agents (school events, appointments) auto-appear on the family calendar.
- **US-4.5**: As a user, I get smart rescheduling suggestions when conflicts are detected.

#### Epic 5: School Event Management
- **US-5.1**: As a user, the School Event Hub agent scans my email/school portals for upcoming events and deadlines.
- **US-5.2**: As a user, I can digitally sign permission slips from within the app.
- **US-5.3**: As a user, I can pay school fees (field trips, supplies) directly from the permission slip card.
- **US-5.4**: As a user, after-school activities are tracked in a timeline view so I can see my kids' full schedule.

#### Epic 6: Budget & Financial Health
- **US-6.1**: As a user, I can scan receipts with my camera and the Budget Buddy agent auto-categorizes expenses.
- **US-6.2**: As a user, I see a "Household Health" dashboard with spending trends and savings wins.
- **US-6.3**: As a user, I get proactive alerts for unused subscriptions and lower utility rates.
- **US-6.4**: As a user, I see upcoming recurring payments ("Recurring Pulse") so nothing catches me off guard.

#### Epic 7: Family Wellness
- **US-7.1**: As a user, I can set appointment reminders for family members (dentist, checkups, etc.).
- **US-7.2**: As a user, I can track wellness streaks (Vitamin D, exercise, water intake) for encouragement.
- **US-7.3**: As a user, I receive proactive wellness nudges based on family activity patterns.
- **US-7.4**: As a user, appointment reminders auto-sync to the Family Calendar.

#### Epic 8: Education & Tutoring
- **US-8.1**: As a user, I can search for tutors filtered by grade level, subject, session type, and specialty (e.g., dyslexia).
- **US-8.2**: As a user, I see community ratings and reviews for each tutor.
- **US-8.3**: As a user, I can book an intro session directly from the tutor card.

#### Epic 9: Tasks & Notifications
- **US-9.1**: As a user, I see real-time agent task progress on the Tasks Dashboard with step-by-step indicators.
- **US-9.2**: As a user, I receive categorized, actionable notifications (NEW/EARLIER) with one-tap actions.
- **US-9.3**: As a user, I get a "Daily Edit" summary of all agent activity each morning.

#### Epic 10: Profile & Settings
- **US-10.1**: As a user, I can manage family members with photos, ages, and custom tags (dietary, learning needs).
- **US-10.2**: As a user, I can set communication style preferences so agents match my tone.
- **US-10.3**: As a user, I have full control over privacy, notification preferences, and connected devices.

## Section 5: Functional Requirements

### FR-1: Authentication & User Management

| ID | Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|
| FR-1.1 | OAuth 2.0 login via Google and Apple Sign-In | P0 |
| FR-1.2 | Email/password registration with email verification | P0 |
| FR-1.3 | Family profile creation: add members with name, age, photo, tags | P0 |
| FR-1.4 | Premium trial activation (14-day) on signup | P0 |
| FR-1.5 | Session management with secure token refresh | P0 |
| FR-1.6 | Multi-device sync (phone, tablet, web) with conflict resolution | P1 |

### FR-2: Agent Marketplace

| ID | Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|
| FR-2.1 | Agent discovery with search and category filtering | P0 |
| FR-2.2 | "Suggested for You" recommendation engine based on family profile | P1 |
| FR-2.3 | Agent activation/deactivation toggle | P0 |
| FR-2.4 | Agent detail cards with description, capabilities, ratings | P0 |
| FR-2.5 | Horizontal carousel for featured agents | P1 |

### FR-3: Agent Execution (Deterministic + Intelligent Split)

Agent operations are split into two tiers to minimize LLM costs. **Deterministic operations** (CRUD, scheduling, lookups) execute instantly with zero LLM calls. **Intelligent operations** (planning, analysis, NLU on unstructured data) use the LLM router.

#### FR-3A: Deterministic Operations (No LLM — Instant, Free)

| ID | Requirement | Priority | Examples |
|---|---|---|---|
| FR-3A.1 | CRUD operations on family data (add/edit/delete members, lists, events) | P0 | "Add milk to grocery list", "Update Maya's age" |
| FR-3A.2 | Calendar queries and event creation | P0 | "What's on tomorrow?", "Add dentist at 3pm Thursday" |
| FR-3A.3 | Reminder creation and scheduling | P0 | "Remind me to pack lunches at 7am" |
| FR-3A.4 | List management (grocery, to-do, shopping) | P0 | "Show my grocery list", "Check off eggs" |
| FR-3A.5 | Notification delivery and preference management | P0 | Push notifications, quiet hours |
| FR-3A.6 | Payment status lookups and recurring pulse display | P0 | "When is rent due?", "Show upcoming bills" |
| FR-3A.7 | Wellness streak tracking (increment/display) | P0 | "Log vitamin D today", "Show Maya's streak" |
| FR-3A.8 | Appointment reminder scheduling + calendar sync | P0 | "Schedule dentist reminder for Tuesday" |
| FR-3A.9 | Permission slip status tracking | P0 | "Which slips are pending?" |
| FR-3A.10 | Tutor search with structured filters | P1 | "Find math tutors for grade 2" |

**Implementation**: Intent classifier (lightweight, rule-based or tiny model) detects deterministic intents → routes to DB operations directly. No LLM call consumed.

#### FR-3B: Intelligent Operations (LLM Required — Counts Against Call Budget)

| ID | Requirement | Priority | Examples |
|---|---|---|---|
| FR-3B.1 | Meal planning with dietary constraints | P0 | "Plan dinners for the week, Leo has peanut allergy" |
| FR-3B.2 | Receipt OCR + expense categorization (vision model) | P0 | Scan receipt photo → extract items, amounts, categorize |
| FR-3B.3 | School email/flyer parsing (unstructured → structured) | P0 | Extract events, deadlines, fees from school newsletter |
| FR-3B.4 | Calendar conflict resolution suggestions | P1 | "Maya has soccer and Leo has doctor at same time — options?" |
| FR-3B.5 | Daily Edit summary generation | P1 | Morning digest of all agent activity across family |
| FR-3B.6 | Budget analysis and savings recommendations | P1 | "You spent 30% more on dining this month — here's why" |
| FR-3B.7 | Recipe suggestions from available ingredients | P1 | "What can I make with chicken, rice, and broccoli?" |
| FR-3B.8 | Natural language complex queries | P1 | "Compare this month's spending to last month" |
| FR-3B.9 | Proactive wellness nudges (pattern analysis) | P2 | "Maya hasn't had outdoor time in 3 days" |
| FR-3B.10 | Tutor match recommendations (personalized) | P2 | "Based on Leo's learning style, here are best matches" |

**Implementation**: LLM Router selects cheapest capable model per request (Gemini Flash → GPT-4o mini → GPT-4o). Each call decrements the household's monthly budget (1,000 Family / 2,000 Pro).

#### FR-3C: Agent Infrastructure

| ID | Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|
| FR-3C.1 | Concurrent execution of 8 agents per household | P0 |
| FR-3C.2 | Shared family context accessible to all agents (calendar, members, preferences) | P0 |
| FR-3C.3 | Per-agent chat interface with natural language input | P0 |
| FR-3C.4 | Intent classifier to route deterministic vs intelligent operations | P0 |
| FR-3C.5 | LLM Router with model selection (Gemini Flash / GPT-4o mini / GPT-4o) | P0 |
| FR-3C.6 | Monthly call budget tracking per household (1,000 / 2,000) | P0 |
| FR-3C.7 | Graceful downgrade to Gemini Flash when over budget | P0 |
| FR-3C.8 | Call usage display in app ("X of 1,000 calls used") | P0 |
| FR-3C.9 | Quick action chips contextual to agent responses | P0 |
| FR-3C.10 | Agent thinking/typing indicator with animation | P1 |
| FR-3C.11 | Rich media responses (images, documents, charts) in chat | P1 |
| FR-3C.12 | Agent-to-agent event bus for cross-domain coordination | P1 |
| FR-3C.13 | Voice input (speech-to-text) for agent interaction | P2 |

## Section 6: Non-Functional Requirements

### NFR-1: Performance

| ID | Requirement | Target |
|---|---|---|
| NFR-1.1 | Agent chat response time (P95) | < 2 seconds |
| NFR-1.2 | UI interaction latency | < 500ms |
| NFR-1.3 | Calendar sync latency | < 5 seconds |
| NFR-1.4 | Receipt OCR processing | < 10 seconds |
| NFR-1.5 | App cold start time | < 3 seconds |
| NFR-1.6 | Concurrent LLM calls per household | Up to 3 parallel |

### NFR-2: Security & Privacy

| ID | Requirement | Standard |
|---|---|---|
| NFR-2.1 | Data encryption at rest and in transit | AES-256 + TLS 1.3 |
| NFR-2.2 | Child data protection | COPPA compliance |
| NFR-2.3 | PII masking in logs and analytics | OWASP LLM Top 10 |
| NFR-2.4 | OAuth token management | RFC 6749 / PKCE |
| NFR-2.5 | Data residency | US initially, EU expansion ready |
| NFR-2.6 | Right to deletion (GDPR/CCPA) | < 30 day fulfillment |
| NFR-2.7 | LLM prompt injection protection | Input sanitization + guard rails |

### NFR-3: Scalability

| ID | Requirement | Target |
|---|---|---|
| NFR-3.1 | Year 1 capacity | 100K households |
| NFR-3.2 | Year 3 capacity | 1M households |
| NFR-3.3 | Agents per household | 9+ concurrent |
| NFR-3.4 | LLM calls per household per day | ~50 avg |
| NFR-3.5 | Real-time sync devices per household | 2-6 |

### NFR-4: Reliability

| ID | Requirement | Target |
|---|---|---|
| NFR-4.1 | System uptime | 99.9% |
| NFR-4.2 | Data durability | 99.999% |
| NFR-4.3 | Graceful degradation on LLM provider outage | Fallback to cached responses |
| NFR-4.4 | Offline support for core features | Service Worker caches calendar + lists; deterministic ops queued for sync |

### NFR-5: Accessibility

| ID | Requirement | Standard |
|---|---|---|
| NFR-5.1 | WCAG 2.1 AA compliance | All screens |
| NFR-5.2 | VoiceOver/TalkBack support | iOS and Android |
| NFR-5.3 | High contrast typography | Enforced by "Lullaby & Logic" design system |
| NFR-5.4 | Minimum touch target size | 44x44pt |

### NFR-6: Internationalization

| ID | Requirement | Target |
|---|---|---|
| NFR-6.1 | English (US) at launch | v1.0 |
| NFR-6.2 | Spanish (US) support | v1.1 |
| NFR-6.3 | RTL layout support | v2.0 |

---

## Section 7: Design & UX Requirements

### Design System: "Lullaby & Logic"

**Creative North Star**: "The Digital Sanctuary" — the app should feel like stepping into a calm, high-end lifestyle magazine that manages life, not a chore tracker.

#### Color Palette
| Token | Value | Usage |
|---|---|---|
| `primary` | #32695a (Grounded Teal) | Main actions, primary UI elements |
| `secondary` | #8f4f14 (Warm Amber) | Secondary interactions, accents |
| `tertiary` | #655883 (Serene Lavender) | Wellness, self-care features |
| `surface-container-lowest` | #ffffff | Base layer |
| `surface-container-low` | #e5f6ff | Card backgrounds |
| `surface-container` | #d7f2ff | Elevated cards |
| `surface-container-high` | #c8eeff | Active states |
| `surface-container-highest` | #b8eaff | Input fields, high-priority |

#### Typography
| Level | Font | Size | Usage |
|---|---|---|---|
| Display-LG | Plus Jakarta Sans | 3.5rem | Celebrations, empty states |
| Headline-MD | Plus Jakarta Sans | 1.75rem | Daily headers, section titles |
| Body-LG | Be Vietnam Pro | 1rem (line-height 1.6) | Primary content |
| Label-MD | Be Vietnam Pro | 0.875rem | Input labels, metadata |

#### Critical Design Rules
1. **"No-Line" Rule**: Zero 1px solid borders. Separation via background color shifts only.
2. **"Glass & Gradient" Rule**: Floating elements use glassmorphism (20-40px backdrop blur).
3. **Tonal Layering**: Depth via stacking surface levels, not drop shadows.
4. **Ambient Shadows**: Extra-diffused only (`blur: 24px, rgba(0,55,71,0.06)`), never pure black.
5. **Signature Gradient**: Hero sections use `linear-gradient(135deg, #32695a, #afe9d7)`.
6. **"Mom-Moment" Chip**: Custom component using `tertiary_container` with soft pebble shape.
7. **Spacing**: Base 0.35rem scale, "Sanctuary Gap" (5.5-7rem) for major section separation.

#### Navigation Structure
- **Top Bar**: Fixed — logo, title, notification/filter/menu icons
- **Bottom Nav**: Fixed — 4 tabs (Home, Tasks, Calendar, Profile) with active indicator
- **Mobile-first**: 390px primary, responsive at md:768px and lg:1024px

#### Screen-Specific Patterns
- **Bento Grid Layout**: Asymmetric multi-column cards (7+5 col featured layout)
- **Horizontal Carousels**: `no-scrollbar` class, grid-based responsive cards
- **Progress Indicators**: Stepper dots (SCAN → MATCH → FINALIZE), segmented bars
- **Hero Sections**: Gradient background + asymmetric text + floating glass panels

---

## Section 8: Technical Architecture & Constraints

### Recommended Technology Stack

#### Frontend
| Component | Recommendation | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Framework | Next.js (PWA) | Mobile-first web app, installable, zero platform fees, design exports are already Tailwind |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS | Direct match to existing HTML prototypes — no conversion needed |
| State Management | Zustand + SWR | Lightweight, great for real-time sync patterns |
| Routing | Next.js App Router | File-based routing, SSR/SSG, built-in API routes |
| PWA | next-pwa + Service Worker | Installable, offline support, Web Push notifications |

#### Backend
| Component | Recommendation | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Database | Supabase (PostgreSQL + Realtime) | Real-time sync, row-level security, auth built-in |
| Auth | Supabase Auth + Google/Apple OAuth | Reduces auth complexity, PKCE support |
| File Storage | Supabase Storage (S3-compatible) | Receipt images, document uploads |
| Edge Functions | Supabase Edge Functions (Deno) | Serverless compute for agent orchestration |

#### AI & Agent Layer
| Component | Recommendation | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Agent Orchestration | LangGraph | Stateful multi-agent workflows, Python-native |
| Primary LLM | GPT-4o (multimodal) | Vision for receipts/documents, fast, cost-effective |
| Fallback LLM | Claude Sonnet 4.6 | Superior reasoning for complex agent tasks |
| Embeddings | Voyage AI | High-quality, cost-effective for family context retrieval |
| Vector Store | Pinecone Serverless | Family context, conversation history, preference matching |
| OCR | GPT-4o Vision | Receipt/document scanning without separate OCR service |
| Voice | OpenAI Realtime API (Whisper) | Speech-to-text for voice input in agent chat |

#### Infrastructure
| Component | Recommendation | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Push Notifications | Web Push API + VAPID | No FCM dependency, works on iOS 16.4+ Safari, Android full support, $0 cost |
| Payments | Stripe | Subscriptions + one-time payments + Connect for tutors |
| Calendar Sync | Google Calendar API + Apple CalDAV | Bidirectional sync with major calendars |
| Banking | Plaid | Receipt categorization, bank account linking |
| Observability | LangSmith + Langfuse | LLM call tracing, agent performance monitoring |
| CI/CD | GitHub Actions + Cloudflare Pages | Auto-deploy on push, preview per PR |
| Monitoring | Sentry (web) + Grafana Cloud | Error tracking + infrastructure metrics |
| Hosting | Cloudflare Pages (frontend) | Global CDN, $0 cost, instant deploys |

### Architecture Constraints
1. **Offline-capable**: Core read operations cached via Service Worker + IndexedDB
2. **Multi-tenant**: Family data isolated at database level (RLS policies)
3. **LLM cost ceiling**: Max $0.50/household/day in LLM costs at scale
4. **Agent isolation**: Agent failures must not cascade to other agents
5. **Health data**: Separate encrypted storage with audit logging
6. **COPPA**: No direct data collection from children under 13

---

## Section 9: Acceptance Criteria & Test Plan

### AC-1: Onboarding Flow
- [ ] User completes Google OAuth signup in < 60 seconds
- [ ] Family profile with 2+ members created during onboarding
- [ ] At least 1 agent recommended and activated by end of onboarding
- [ ] Premium trial automatically starts (14-day countdown visible)
- [ ] User lands on Home/Marketplace after onboarding

### AC-2: Agent Marketplace
- [ ] All 8 agents displayed with cards showing name, description, category
- [ ] Search filters agents by name and category
- [ ] "Suggested for You" section shows personalized recommendations
- [ ] Agent activation toggle works and immediately reflects in Tasks Dashboard

### AC-3: Agent Chat & Deterministic/Intelligent Routing
- [ ] Deterministic operations (add to list, set reminder, view calendar) respond in < 200ms with zero LLM calls
- [ ] Intelligent operations (meal planning, receipt OCR, email parsing) route to LLM and respond within 2 seconds (P95)
- [ ] Intent classifier correctly routes ≥ 90% of messages to the right tier (deterministic vs intelligent)
- [ ] Agent typing indicator shows only during LLM calls (deterministic ops are instant)
- [ ] Quick action chips appear contextually after agent responses
- [ ] Rich media (images, documents) renders inline in chat
- [ ] Voice input (speech-to-text) transcribes and sends correctly
- [ ] LLM call count increments only for intelligent operations, not deterministic ones

### AC-4: Family Calendar
- [ ] Monthly and weekly views render correctly with toggle
- [ ] Events from Google Calendar appear within 5 seconds of sync
- [ ] Per-member color coding displays correctly
- [ ] AI conflict detection surfaces warning when same member has overlapping events
- [ ] Agent-created events (School Event Hub, Health Hub) auto-appear

### AC-5: School Event Hub
- [ ] School emails parsed and events extracted within 30 seconds
- [ ] Permission slip card shows "Sign Slip" CTA
- [ ] E-signature captured and stored securely
- [ ] Payment processing completes via Stripe for school fees
- [ ] Synced events appear on Family Calendar

### AC-6: Budget Buddy
- [ ] Receipt photo captured and OCR extracts merchant, amount, date, items
- [ ] Expense auto-categorized (Groceries, Kids, Home) with > 85% accuracy
- [ ] "Household Health" dashboard shows 6-bar sparkline trend
- [ ] "Recurring Pulse" lists upcoming payments sorted by date
- [ ] Savings recommendations appear when patterns detected

### AC-7: Family Wellness Hub
- [ ] Appointment reminders created and displayed per family member
- [ ] Appointment reminders auto-sync to Family Calendar
- [ ] Wellness streaks (vitamin, exercise, water) track correctly with day grid
- [ ] Proactive wellness nudges surface based on activity patterns

### AC-8: Notifications
- [ ] Push notifications delivered within 10 seconds of agent event
- [ ] Notification Center groups by NEW / EARLIER
- [ ] Action buttons in notifications execute correctly (Sign Slip, View, Pay)
- [ ] Quiet hours suppress non-critical notifications
- [ ] "Daily Edit" summary delivered each morning at user-configured time

### AC-9: Security & Privacy
- [ ] All user data encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3)
- [ ] COPPA: no PII collected from users under 13 without verifiable parental consent
- [ ] User can export all personal data (GDPR/CCPA)
- [ ] User can delete account and all data within 30 days
- [ ] LLM prompts sanitized against injection attacks
- [ ] OAuth tokens refreshed securely, never stored in plain text

### AC-10: Subscription & Call Budget
- [ ] 14-day free trial starts with CC on file; full Family tier (1,000 calls) accessible
- [ ] Trial converts to paid Family ($7.99) automatically after 14 days unless cancelled
- [ ] Call budget counter accurately tracks only intelligent (LLM) operations
- [ ] "X of 1,000 calls used" displays correctly in app settings/profile
- [ ] Over-budget households gracefully downgrade to Gemini Flash (no hard cutoff, still functional)
- [ ] Family Pro users see 2,000 call budget and get priority model routing
- [ ] Annual billing ($69.99/$129.99) processes correctly with prorated upgrades/downgrades
- [ ] Post-trial: app locks agent features, data preserved for 30 days, resubscribe to resume
- [ ] Call budget resets on billing cycle date, not calendar month

### AC-11: Performance & Scale
- [ ] App cold start < 3 seconds on iPhone 12 / Pixel 6 equivalents
- [ ] Agent chat P95 latency < 2 seconds
- [ ] Calendar sync completes in < 5 seconds
- [ ] System handles 100K concurrent households without degradation
- [ ] Offline mode: calendar and cached task data viewable without network

---

## Appendix A: Glossary

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| Agent | A specialized AI module that manages one domain of family life (e.g., budgeting, health). Handles both deterministic and intelligent operations. |
| Deterministic Operation | An agent action that requires no LLM call — direct DB query, CRUD, scheduling. Instant, free, unlimited. |
| Intelligent Operation | An agent action requiring LLM reasoning — planning, OCR, parsing, analysis. Counts against monthly call budget. |
| LLM Router | Backend component that selects the cheapest capable model (Gemini Flash → GPT-4o mini → GPT-4o) for each intelligent operation. |
| Call Budget | Monthly limit on intelligent operations per household (1,000 Family / 2,000 Pro). Over-budget = Gemini Flash only. |
| Intent Classifier | Lightweight rule-based + tiny model system that routes user messages to deterministic or intelligent execution paths. |
| Family Context | Shared data (members, preferences, schedule) accessible to all agents |
| Mom Moment | Wellness micro-break suggested by the Self-Care Reminder agent |
| Daily Edit | Morning summary notification aggregating all agent activity |
| Sanctuary Gap | Large whitespace (5.5-7rem) in the design system for visual breathing room |
| Recurring Pulse | Budget Buddy's upcoming payments monitoring feature |
| Family Wellness Hub | Appointment reminders, wellness streak tracking, and general wellness nudges (no medical data) |
| Household Health | Budget Buddy's financial wellness dashboard |
| Active Sync | Real-time connection indicator for school portal scanning |

## Appendix B: Competitive Landscape

| Competitor | Category | Mom.Ai Differentiator |
|---|---|---|
| Cozi | Family organizer | No AI, no proactive management, no agent ecosystem |
| FamilyWall | Family calendar/lists | Limited to calendar + lists, no intelligence |
| Mint/YNAB | Budgeting | Siloed, no family context, no cross-domain coordination |
| ClassDojo | School communication | One-directional, no automation, no payment processing |
| Google Assistant | AI assistant | Stateless, no persistent family context, no specialized domains |
| Apple Family | Device management | Hardware-focused, no household management intelligence |

## Appendix C: Risk Register

| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| LLM cost overrun at scale | High | High | Token budgets per agent, caching, smaller models for simple tasks |
| School portal access fragmentation | Medium | High | Start with email parsing; portal APIs as partnerships grow |
| COPPA compliance for child profiles | Medium | High | Verifiable parental consent flow, no direct child accounts |
| User trust in AI managing family | Medium | Medium | Transparent agent actions, human-in-loop for critical decisions |
| Multi-LLM provider dependency | Low | Medium | Abstraction layer, fallback providers, graceful degradation |
